# Stroke Admission Orders

and Workup: *exceptions to guidelines if tPA given
- For routine stroke admissions, use order set “NEU STROKE PROGRAM ISCHEMIC STROKE ADMISSION” *Post-tPA or mechanical thrombectomy patients use order set “NEU STROKE PROGRAM ORDERS – POST ALTEPLASE (T-PA) ADMISSION ORDERS IP UF”
- Bedside dysphagia screen performed by nursing, must be documented in chart. If the patient fails, he/she MUST have formal swallow evaluation.
- Patient who fail dysphagia screen must be strict NPO (this includes oral medications!) until they are cleared by speech/swallow
- Make sure any medication orders accurately reflect the patient’s po status (eg oral or DHT route)
- ASA 325 mg PO if swallowing, 300 mg PR if not (*unless on anticoagulation or tPA in last 24 hours)
- Statin
- NS @ 100 cc/hr (careful with patients with CHF or on HD). Avoid hypotonic fluids.
- acetaminophen 650 mg q4h prn temp >100.4
- sliding scale insulin and fingersticks q6h
- neurochecks q4h on floor (q2h in IMC, q1h in NICU)
- UA/UDS
- CXR
- EKG in ED, telemetry/monitoring after admission
- SCD (all) plus Heparin SQ 5,000 units q8h (*unless tPA in last 24 hours)
- Permissive hypertension (SBP<220, DBP<110), hold home BP meds (except ½ dose β-blocker) o Exception: BP goal will be lower following IV tPA (<180/105) or mechanical thrombectomy (goal depends on procedure result, discuss with fellow/attending)
- Labs: lipid panel, HgbA1c, RPR, liver function tests, troponin, TSH, folate/B12
- If concerned for endocarditis, send serial blood cultures
- Transthoracic echocardiogram (+/- bubble study if <60 years old)
- MRI brain
- MRA head and neck vs. carotid ultrasound/transcranial dopplers if patient has not had CTA head/neck **Certain patients (e.g., younger patients) may require TEE, hypercoagulable or vasculitis workup. Discuss with attending before ordering. Hypercoagulable panel: Antiphospholipid panel (anticardiolipin, DRVVT, beta-2 microglobulin, prothrombin gene mutation, Factor V Leiden mutation, Protein C activity, Protein S activity, antithrombin III activity, HIT antibody (in appropriate clinical setting).
